from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage, QColor
from core.adjustments.hdr_toning import apply_hdr_toning, HDRToningDialog

img = QImage\(40, 30, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
img.fill\(QColor\(120, 80, 200\)\)

out = apply_hdr_toning\(img, radius=5, strength=0.6, gamma=1.2, detail=0.3\)
print\('apply OK, format:', out.format\(\)\)

# Check caching: simulate dialog usage
import numpy as np
from ui.adjustments_dialog import _bits_ba, _to_argb32
src = _to_argb32\(img\)
ba, arr = _bits_ba\(src\)
orig_f = arr[:,:,:3].astype\(np.float32\)/255.0

from core.adjustments.hdr_toning import _gauss_blur, _hdr_combine
blurred = _gauss_blur\(orig_f, 5\)
print\('blur shape:', blurred.shape, 'dtype:', blurred.dtype\)
res = _hdr_combine\(np, orig_f, blurred, 0.5, 1.0, 0.2\)
print\('combine shape:', res.shape, 'dtype:', res.dtype\)
print\('All OK'\)
")

from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage, QColor
import sys
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

# Test the blur on a real image
img = QImage\(200, 200, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
img.fill\(QColor\(128, 64, 32\)\)

from core.filters.blur_filters import apply_gaussian_blur
for r in \(0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 50.0, 250.0\):
    out = apply_gaussian_blur\(img, r\)
    assert out.width\(\) == 200 and out.height\(\) == 200, f'bad size at r={r}'
    print\(f'  radius={r:5.1f} -> OK'\)

print\('All OK'\)
app.quit\(\)

import time
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage, QColor
import sys
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

img = QImage\(800, 600, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
img.fill\(QColor\(100, 150, 200\)\)

from core.filters.blur_filters import apply_smart_blur
from core.filters.motion_blur import apply_motion_blur
from core.filters.radial_blur import apply_radial_blur

tests = [
    \('Smart Blur r=50  t=30',  lambda: apply_smart_blur\(img, 50, 30\)\),
    \('Smart Blur r=100 t=15',  lambda: apply_smart_blur\(img, 100, 15\)\),
    \('Motion Blur s=500',      lambda: apply_motion_blur\(img, 45, 500\)\),
    \('Motion Blur s=999',      lambda: apply_motion_blur\(img, 30, 999\)\),
    \('Radial Spin a=100',      lambda: apply_radial_blur\(img, 'Spin', 100\)\),
    \('Radial Zoom a=100',      lambda: apply_radial_blur\(img, 'Zoom', 100\)\),
]
for name, fn in tests:
    t0 = time.perf_counter\(\)
    out = fn\(\)
    dt = time.perf_counter\(\) - t0
    assert out.width\(\) == 800 and out.height\(\) == 600
    print\(f'  {name:26s} {dt*1000:5.0f} ms'\)
app.quit\(\)
" 2>&1)

import json
with open\('locales/en.json'\) as f:
    en = json.load\(f\)
with open\('locales/ru.json'\) as f:
    ru = json.load\(f\)

ru_keys = set\(ru\)

missing_en = ru_keys - en_keys
if missing_ru:
    print\('Missing in ru.json:', missing_ru\)

import sys
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint, QRect
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

# Simulate ShapesTool behavior
from tools.other_tools import ShapesTool
tool = ShapesTool\(\)

# Mock doc and layer
class FakeLayer:
    locked = False
    image = QImage\(100, 100, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
class FakeDoc:
    selection = None
    def get_active_layer\(self\): return FakeLayer\(\)

doc = FakeDoc\(\)
fg = QColor\(255, 0, 0\)
bg = QColor\(255, 255, 255\)
opts = {'brush_size': 5, 'shape_type': 'rect'}

tool.on_press\(QPoint\(10, 10\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('after press: start=', tool._start, 'preview_end=', tool._preview_end\)
tool.on_move\(QPoint\(50, 50\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('after move: preview_shape=', tool.preview_shape\(\)\)
tool.on_release\(QPoint\(60, 60\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('after release: start=', tool._start, 'preview_end=', tool._preview_end\)
print\('SUCCESS'\)
" 2>&1)

import sys
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

from tools.other_tools import ShapesTool
tool = ShapesTool\(\)

layer_img = QImage\(100, 100, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
layer_img.fill\(0xFFFFFFFF\)  # white

class FakeLayer:
    locked = False
    image = layer_img
class FakeDoc:
    selection = None
    def get_active_layer\(self\): return FakeLayer\(\)

doc = FakeDoc\(\)
fg = QColor\(255, 0, 0\)  # red
opts = {'brush_size': 5, 'shape_type': 'rect'}

# pixel before
print\('pixel before \(50,50\):', hex\(layer_img.pixel\(50, 50\)\)\)

tool.on_press\(QPoint\(10, 10\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
tool.on_move\(QPoint\(50, 50\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
tool.on_release\(QPoint\(60, 60\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)

# pixel after  
print\('pixel after  \(10,40\):', hex\(layer_img.pixel\(10, 40\)\)\)
print\('pixel after  \(10,10\):', hex\(layer_img.pixel\(10, 10\)\)\)
print\('pixel after  \(60,60\):', hex\(layer_img.pixel\(60, 60\)\)\)
" 2>&1)

import sys
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

from tools.other_tools import ShapesTool
original_on_release = ShapesTool.on_release

def debug_on_release\(self, pos, doc, fg, bg, opts\):
    print\(f'on_release called: _start={self._start}, pos={pos}'\)
    original_on_release\(self, pos, doc, fg, bg, opts\)
    print\(f'on_release done: _start={self._start}'\)

ShapesTool.on_release = debug_on_release

# Now simulate canvas behavior
from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage
from PyQt6.QtCore import QRect

tool = ShapesTool\(\)

class FakeLayer:
    locked = False
    image = QImage\(200, 200, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
    
class FakeDoc:
    selection = None
    def get_active_layer\(self\): return FakeLayer\(\)

doc = FakeDoc\(\)
fg = QColor\(255, 0, 0\)
opts = {'brush_size': 5, 'shape_type': 'rect'}

# Simulate press at position 0,0 \(top-left — potential bool issue!\)
tool.on_press\(QPoint\(0, 0\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
print\('_start =', tool._start, '  bool\(_start\) =', bool\(tool._start\)\)
tool.on_move\(QPoint\(50, 50\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
tool.on_release\(QPoint\(60, 60\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
print\(\)

# Simulate press at normal position
tool.on_press\(QPoint\(10, 10\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
print\('_start =', tool._start, '  bool\(_start\) =', bool\(tool._start\)\)
tool.on_move\(QPoint\(50, 50\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
tool.on_release\(QPoint\(60, 60\), doc, fg, QColor\(\), opts\)
" 2>&1)

import sys
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint, QPointF
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

from tools.other_tools import ShapesTool
from core.document import Document

# Create a real document
doc = Document\(200, 200\)
tool = ShapesTool\(\)
fg = QColor\(255, 0, 0\)
bg = QColor\(255, 255, 255\)
opts = {'brush_size': 5, 'shape_type': 'rect'}

layer = doc.get_active_layer\(\)
print\('Before: pixel at \(30,10\):', hex\(layer.image.pixel\(30, 10\)\)\)

# Simulate: press at \(10,10\), move to \(50,50\), release at \(60,60\)
tool.on_press\(QPoint\(10, 10\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('preview_shape after press:', tool.preview_shape\(\)\)
tool.on_move\(QPoint\(50, 50\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('preview_shape after move:', tool.preview_shape\(\)\)
tool.on_release\(QPoint\(60, 60\), doc, fg, bg, opts\)
print\('preview_shape after release:', tool.preview_shape\(\)\)

print\('After: pixel at \(10,40\):', hex\(layer.image.pixel\(10, 40\)\)\)
print\('After: pixel at \(60,10\):', hex\(layer.image.pixel\(60, 10\)\)\)

keys = [
    'layer.name.artboard',
    'opts.shape.rect','opts.shape.ellipse','opts.gradient.reverse','opts.shadow',
    'opts.apply','opts.stroke_abbr','opts.load','opts.pattern','opts.pattern_scale',

import json

    ru = json.load\(f\)
with open\('/home/lucial/Git Projects/linuxphotoshop/locales/en.json'\) as f:

keys = [
    'layer.name.artboard',
    'opts.link_aspect','opts.clear_slices','opts.cancel',
    'opts.align.left','opts.align.center_h','opts.align.right',
    'opts.align.top','opts.align.center_v','opts.align.bottom',

]

    r = ru.get\(k\)

        print\(f'  {k}'\)

if ok:
    print\('All keys present in both files!'\)

Bash(cd "/home/lucial/Git Projects/linuxphotoshop" && python3 -c "
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage, QPainter
import sys

app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

from core.document import Document
from ui.canvas_widget import CanvasWidget

doc = Document\(400, 300, QColor\(255,255,255\)\)
doc.show_grid = True

canvas = CanvasWidget\(\)
canvas.resize\(800, 600\)
canvas.set_document\(doc\)
canvas.show\(\)

# Force a paint to a QImage to check no exceptions
img = QImage\(800, 600, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32\)
img.fill\(0\)
p = QPainter\(img\)
canvas._paint_canvas_content\(p\)
p.end\(\)

# Check pixel at grid position
# Grid is at doc coords 0,50,100,... scaled and translated
# At zoom~1 and pan ~\(200,150\), pixel at pan_x+50 should differ from pan_x+25
px = img.pixel\(200, 150\)
py = img.pixel\(250, 150\)  # 50 doc coords offset
print\('No exception in _paint_canvas_content: OK'\)
print\(f'Pixel at pan origin: {px:#010x}'\)
print\(f'Pixel at +50 offset: {py:#010x}'\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(cd "/home/lucial/Git Projects/linuxphotoshop" && timeout 8 python3 -c "
import sys
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage, QPainter, QColor
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)

from ui.main_window import MainWindow
w = MainWindow\(\)
w.show\(\)
app.processEvents\(\)

canvas = w._canvas
doc = w._document

# Enable grid
doc.show_grid = True
app.processEvents\(\)

# Render to image
img = QImage\(canvas.size\(\), QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
img.fill\(QColor\(30, 30, 40\)\)
p = QPainter\(img\)
canvas._paint_canvas_content\(p\)
p.end\(\)

# Find pan and zoom
pan = canvas._pan
zoom = canvas.zoom
print\(f'pan={pan.x\(\):.0f},{pan.y\(\):.0f}  zoom={zoom:.2f}'\)

# Check pixel at doc position \(50,50\) -- should be on grid line
wx = int\(pan.x\(\) + 50 * zoom\)
wy = int\(pan.y\(\) + 50 * zoom\)
px_on = img.pixel\(wx, wy\)
px_off = img.pixel\(wx + 10, wy + 10\)  # off grid
print\(f'On grid \({wx},{wy}\): {px_on:#010x}'\)
print\(f'Off grid \({wx+10},{wy+10}\): {px_off:#010x}'\)
print\('Grid visible:', px_on != px_off\)
" 2>&1)

# Второй вызов должен вернуть из кэша
stamp2 = p._get_stamp\(20, 1.0, 'round'\)
print\('Same object from cache:', stamp1 is stamp2\)
print\('Cache keys:', list\(p._stamp_cache.keys\(\)\)[:3]\)
print\('PencilTool stamp cache OK'\)
app.quit\(\)
")

# Тест векторизации маски
from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
import sys
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)
from core.document import Document
from core.layer import Layer
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint

doc = Document\(100, 100, QColor\(255, 0, 0\)\)
layer = doc.get_active_layer\(\)

# Создаём маску
mask = QImage\(100, 100, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
mask.fill\(QColor\(128, 128, 128, 255\)\)
layer.mask = mask
layer.mask_enabled = True

comp = doc.get_composite\(\)
# Проверяем что маска применилась \(красный стал ~50% прозрачным\)
import ctypes, numpy as np
ptr = comp.constBits\(\)
buf = \(ctypes.c_uint8 * comp.sizeInBytes\(\)\).from_address\(int\(ptr\)\)
arr = np.ndarray\(\(100, comp.bytesPerLine\(\) // 4, 4\), dtype=np.uint8, buffer=buf\)[:, :100, :]
print\('R channel with 50% mask \(should be ~127\):', arr[50, 50, 2]\)
print\('Alpha with 50% mask \(should be ~127\):', arr[50, 50, 3]\)
print\('Vectorized mask OK'\)
app.quit\(\)
")

from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
import sys
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
from PyQt6.QtCore import QPoint, QPointF
from core.document import Document
from ui.canvas_widget import CanvasWidget
from tools.effect_tools import BlurTool, SmudgeTool, DodgeTool, BurnTool, SharpenTool, SpongeTool
from tools.brush_tool import PencilTool, BrushTool, EraserTool

print\('=== Test 1: effect tools have stroke_preview + needs_background_composite ==='\)
for ToolCls in [BlurTool, SharpenTool, SmudgeTool, DodgeTool, BurnTool, SpongeTool]:
    t = ToolCls\(\)
    assert hasattr\(t, 'stroke_preview'\), f'{ToolCls.__name__} missing stroke_preview'
    assert getattr\(t, 'needs_background_composite', False\), f'{ToolCls.__name__} missing needs_background_composite'
    assert t.stroke_preview\(\) is None, f'{ToolCls.__name__} should return None when inactive'
    print\(f'  {ToolCls.__name__}: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 2: BrushTool does NOT have needs_background_composite ==='\)
for ToolCls in [BrushTool, EraserTool, PencilTool]:
    t = ToolCls\(\)
    assert not getattr\(t, 'needs_background_composite', False\), f'{ToolCls.__name__} should NOT have needs_background_composite'
    print\(f'  {ToolCls.__name__}: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 3: canvas _start_effect_stroke ==='\)
doc = Document\(100, 100, QColor\(255, 0, 0\)\)
cw = CanvasWidget\(\)
cw.set_document\(doc\)
tool = BlurTool\(\)
cw.active_tool = tool

cw._start_effect_stroke\(\)
assert cw._in_effect_stroke == True
assert cw._effect_bg_cache is not None
assert tool._stroke_preview_active == True
print\('  _start_effect_stroke: OK'\)

# stroke_preview должен вернуть layer.image
tool._begin_effect_stroke\(doc\)
sp = tool.stroke_preview\(\)
assert sp is not None and isinstance\(sp[0], QImage\)
print\('  stroke_preview\(\) active: OK'\)

# После окончания — reset
cw.active_tool._stroke_preview_active = False
cw._in_effect_stroke = False
cw._effect_bg_cache = None
assert tool.stroke_preview\(\) is None
print\('  stroke_preview\(\) after reset: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 4: PencilTool stamp cache ==='\)
p = PencilTool\(\)
s1 = p._get_stamp\(30, 1.0, 'round'\)
s2 = p._get_stamp\(30, 1.0, 'round'\)
assert s1 is s2, 'PencilTool should cache stamps'
print\('  PencilTool stamp cache: OK'\)

print\('=== ALL TESTS PASSED ==='\)
app.quit\(\)
")

from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication
import sys
app = QApplication\(sys.argv\)
from PyQt6.QtGui import QColor, QImage
from core.document import Document
from ui.canvas_widget import CanvasWidget
from tools.effect_tools import BlurTool, SharpenTool, SmudgeTool, DodgeTool, BurnTool, SpongeTool
from tools.brush_tool import PencilTool, BrushTool, EraserTool

print\('=== Test 1: effect tools ==='\)
for ToolCls in [BlurTool, SharpenTool, SmudgeTool, DodgeTool, BurnTool, SpongeTool]:
    t = ToolCls\(\)
    assert hasattr\(t, 'stroke_preview'\)
    assert getattr\(t, 'needs_background_composite', False\)
    assert t.stroke_preview\(\) is None
    print\(f'  {ToolCls.__name__}: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 2: BrushTool no needs_background_composite ==='\)
for ToolCls in [BrushTool, EraserTool, PencilTool]:
    t = ToolCls\(\)
    assert not getattr\(t, 'needs_background_composite', False\)
    print\(f'  {ToolCls.__name__}: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 3: _start_effect_stroke flow ==='\)
doc = Document\(100, 100, QColor\(255, 0, 0\)\)
cw = CanvasWidget\(\)
cw.set_document\(doc\)
tool = BlurTool\(\)
cw.active_tool = tool

# Симуляция порядка в mousePressEvent:
# 1. _start_effect_stroke\(\) — ДО on_press
cw._start_effect_stroke\(\)
assert cw._in_effect_stroke == True
assert tool._stroke_preview_active == True  # установлен ЗДЕСЬ

# 2. on_press\(\) — вызывает _begin_effect_stroke внутри
tool.on_press\(doc.get_active_layer\(\).offset, doc, QColor\(0,0,0\), QColor\(255,255,255\), {}\)
# _stroke_preview_active должен остаться True
assert tool._stroke_preview_active == True, f'Expected True, got {tool._stroke_preview_active}'
print\('  _stroke_preview_active stays True after on_press: OK'\)

# 3. stroke_preview\(\) должен работать
sp = tool.stroke_preview\(\)
assert sp is not None
assert isinstance\(sp[0], QImage\)
print\('  stroke_preview\(\) active: OK'\)

# 4. Окончание мазка
tool.on_release\(None, doc, None, None, {}\)
assert tool._stroke_preview_active == False
assert tool._eff_layer_ref is None
print\('  on_release cleanup: OK'\)

print\('=== Test 4: PencilTool stamp cache ==='\)
p = PencilTool\(\)
s1 = p._get_stamp\(30, 1.0, 'round'\)
s2 = p._get_stamp\(30, 1.0, 'round'\)
assert s1 is s2
print\('  PencilTool stamp cache: OK'\)

print\('=== ALL TESTS PASSED ==='\)
app.quit\(\)
")
